Themes

How to understand Srimati Radharani

Lecture Transcription

Sri Radharani, the beloved of Madhava and most worship-able of the cowherd girls of Gokula Mandala! Decorated by the dress of Lord Damodara’s increasing ecstasy, you are the lady of the house of Lord Hari and the groves of Vrindavan. From the ocean of Vrishabhanu has arisen a new moon which is overwhelming the qualities of Lalita and Vishakha. O Goddess, your qualities are described by Sanaka and Sanatana rishis and by Sanatana goswami. Please bestow Your mercy upon me.

rādhā kṛṣṇa-praṇaya-vikṛtir hlādinī śaktir asmād
ekātmānāv api bhuvi purā deha-bhedaṁ gatau tau
caitanyākhyaṁ prakaṭam adhunā tad-dvayaṁ caikyam āptaṁ
rādhā-bhāva-dyuti-suvalitaṁ naumi kṛṣṇa-svarūpam

[CC Adi 1.5]

The loving affairs of Śrī Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are transcendental manifestations of the Lord’s internal pleasure-giving potency. Although Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are one in their identity, they separated themselves eternally. Now these two transcendental identities have again united in the form of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya. I bow down to him, who has manifested himself with the sentiment and complexion of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī although he is Kṛṣṇa himself.

ataeva sei bhāva aṅgīkāra kari’
sādhilena nija vāṣchā gaurāṅga-śrī-hari

[CC Adi 4.50]

Therefore Lord Gauranga, who is Sri Krsna himself, accepted the sentiments of Radha and thus fulfilled his own desires.

Purport by His Divine Grace AC Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada

Of the four kinds of reciprocation of loving service — dāsya, sakhya, vātsalya and mādhurya — mādhurya is considered the fullest. But the conjugal relationship is further divided into two varieties, namely svakīya and parakīya. Svakīya is the relationship with Kṛṣṇa as a formally married husband, and parakīya is the relationship with Kṛṣṇa as a paramour. Expert analysts have decided that the transcendental ecstasy of the parakīya mellow is better because it is more enthusiastic. This phase of conjugal love is found in those who have surrendered to the Lord in intense love, knowing well that such illicit love with a paramour is not morally approved in society. The risks involved in such love of Godhead make this emotion superior to the relationship in which such risk is not involved. The validity of such risk, however, is possible only in the transcendental realm. Svakīya and parakīya conjugal love of Godhead have no existence in the material world, and parakīya is not existed anywhere in Vaikuṇṭha, but only in the portion of Goloka Vṛndāvana known as Vraja.

Some devotees think that Kṛṣṇa is eternally the enjoyer in Goloka Vṛndāvana but only sometimes comes to the platform of Vraja to enjoy parakīya-rasa. The six Gosvāmīs of Vṛndāvana, however, have explained that Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes in Vraja are eternal, like his other activities in Goloka Vṛndāvana. Vraja is a confidential part of Goloka Vṛndāvana. Kṛṣṇa exhibited his Vraja pastimes on the surface of this world, and similar pastimes are eternally exhibited in Vraja in Goloka Vṛndāvana, where parakīya-rasa is ever existent.

In the third chapter of this epic, Śrīla Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja Gosvāmī has explicitly accepted the fact that Kṛṣṇa appears in this material world at the end of the Dvāpara age of the twenty-eighth catur-yuga of Vaivasvata Manu and brings with him his Vrajadhāma, which is the eternal abode of His highest pastimes. As the Lord appears by his own internal potency, so he also brings all his paraphernalia by the same internal potency, without extraneous help. It is further stated here in Caitanya-caritāmṛta that the parakīya sentiment is existent only in that transcendental realm and nowhere else. This highest form of ecstasy can exist only in the most confidential part of the spiritual world, but by the causeless mercy of the Lord, we can have a peep into the invisible Vraja.

The transcendental mellow relished by the gopīs in Vraja is super-excellently featured in Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī. Mature assimilation of the transcendental humor of conjugal love is represented by Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, whose feelings are incomprehensible even to the Lord himself. The intensity of her loving service is the highest form of ecstasy. No one can surpass Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī in relishing the t transcendental qualities of the Lord through this supreme transcendental mellow. Therefore, the Lord himself agreed to assume the position of Rādhārāṇī in the form of Lord Śrī Gaurāṅga. He then relished the highest position of parakīya-rasa, as exhibited in the transcendental abode of Vraja.

rādhā-kṛṣṇa eka ātmā, dui deha dhari’
anyonye vilase rasa āsvādana kari’

(CC Adi 4.56)

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are one and the same, but They have assumed two bodies. Thus, they enjoy each other, tasting the mellows of love.

The two transcendentalists Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are a puzzle to materialists. The above description of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa from the diary of Śrīla Svarūpa Dāmodara Gosvāmī is a condensed explanation, but one needs great spiritual insight to understand the mystery of these two personalities. One is enjoying in two. Śrī Kṛṣṇa is the potent factor, and Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī is the internal potency. According to Vedānta philosophy, there is no difference between the potent and the potency; they are identical. We cannot differentiate between one and the other, any more than we can separate fire from heat.

Everything in the Absolute is inconceivable in relative existence. Therefore, in relative cognizance it is very difficult to assimilate this truth of the oneness between the potent and the potency. The philosophy of inconceivable oneness and difference propounded by Lord Chaitanya is the only source of understanding for such intricacies of transcendence.

In fact, Rādhārāṇī is the internal potency of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, and she eternally intensifies the pleasure of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Impersonalists cannot understand this without the help of a mahā-bhāgavata devotee. The very name “Rādhā” suggests that she is eternally the topmost mistress of the comforts of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. As such, she is the medium transmitting the living entities’ service to Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Devotees in Vṛndāvana therefore seek the mercy of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī in order to be recognized as loving servitors of Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu personally approaches the fallen conditioned souls of the iron age to deliver the highest principle of transcendental relationships with the Lord. The activities of Lord Caitanya are primarily in the role of the pleasure-giving portion of his internal potency.

The absolute Personality of Godhead, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, is the omnipotent form of transcendental existence, knowledge and bliss in full. His internal potency is exhibited first as sat, or existence — or, in other words, as the portion that expands the existence function of the Lord. The same potency while displaying full knowledge, is called cit, or samvit, which expands the transcendental forms of the Lord. Finally, the same potency while playing as a pleasure-giving medium, is known as hlādinī, or the transcendental blissful potency. Thus, the Lord manifests his internal potency in three transcendental divisions.

sei dui eka ebe caitanya gosāṣi
rasa āsvādite doṅhe hailā eka-jāṅi

[CC Adi 4.57]

Now, to enjoy rasa, they have appeared in one body as Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu.

ithi lāgi’ āge kari tāra vivaraṇa
yāhā haite haya gaurera mahimā-kathana

[CC Adi 4.58]

Therefore, I shall first delineate the position of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa. From that, the glory of Lord Caitanya will be known.

rādhikā hayena kṛṣṇera praṇaya-vikāra
svarūpa-śakti — ‘hlādinī’ nāma yāṅhāra

[CC Adi 4.59]

Śrīmatī Rādharani is the transformation of Kṛṣṇa’s love. She is his internal energy called hlādinī.

hlādinī karāya kṛṣṇe ānandāsvādana
hlādinīra dvārā kare bhaktera poṣaṇa

[CC Adi 4.60]

That hlādinī energy gives Kṛṣṇa pleasure and nourishes His devotees.

Purport:

Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī has elaborately discussed the hlādinī potency in his Prīti-sandarbha. He says that the Vedas clearly state, “Only devotional service can lead one to the Personality of Godhead. Only devotional service can help a devotee meet the Supreme Lord face to face. The Supreme Personality of Godhead is attracted by devotional service, and as such the ultimate supremacy of Vedic knowledge rests in knowing the science of devotional service.”

What is the particular attraction that makes the Supreme Lord enthusiastic to accept devotional service, and what is the nature of such service? The Vedic scriptures inform us that the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Absolute Truth, is self-sufficient, and that māyā, nescience, can never influence him at all. As such, the potency that overcomes the Supreme must be purely spiritual. Such a potency cannot be anything of the material manifestation. The bliss enjoyed by the Supreme Personality of Godhead cannot be of material composition, like the impersonalist conception of the bliss of Brahman. Devotional service is reciprocation between two, and therefore it cannot be located simply within one’s self. Therefore, the bliss of self-realization, brahmānanda, cannot be equated to devotional service.

[CC Adi 4.62 Purport] In his thesis Bhagavat-sandarbha (103), Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī explains the potencies of the Lord as follows: The transcendental potency of the Supreme Personality of Godhead by which He maintains His existence is called sandhinī. The transcendental potency by which He knows himself and causes others to know him is called samvit. The transcendental potency by which he possesses transcendental bliss and causes his devotees to have bliss is called hlādinī.

Today, we are celebrating the auspicious appearance of Srimati Radharani, Sri Radhastami.

On this day, it is important that we understand according to the sastra, exactly who Srimati Radharani actually is, otherwise we find that most people, they chant Jai Radhe and they have some sentimental conception of Radha Kṛṣṇa’s leela, but they have no grasp or concept of the spiritual nature of the divine leela of Radha and Kṛṣṇa.

This is called Sahajiya. Sahajiya means to cheaply think that you can enter into the divine leela of Radha and Kṛṣṇa, or to just believe it is beautiful stories that we can just sing and dance about all of the time.

Of course, there is some purification to that process. But if we want to actually understand these top most spiritual subject matters, we cannot approach them with our own sentimental feelings, but we must first understand through the scriptures and through the acharyas of what is the spiritual gravity of the divine leela of Sri Sri Radha and Kṛṣṇa. In fact, Radha and Kṛṣṇa are unapproachable by anyone who has any material attachments within their life.

The divine pastimes of Sri Sri Radha and Kṛṣṇa are the enactments of the pure spiritual bliss of the spiritual world. Therefore, we must approach the pastimes of Radha- Kṛṣṇa not cheaply, but we must approach them through the process of vidhi bhakti, which means through following the strict principles of the acharyas by controlling our senses, by engaging in the humble service of the spiritual master and sadhus and by learning from them gradually, what is spiritual life.

We must first understand the difference between the body and the soul and then we must understand the various features in which Kṛṣṇa appears in this world.

Kṛṣṇa says janma karma ca me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ, we must understand the transcendental nature of Kṛṣṇa’s appearance and activities in this world. And how do we understand? We must understand this subject. Kṛṣṇa explains tad viddhi praṇipātena
paripraśnena sevayā upadekṣyanti te jṣānaṁ jṣāninas tattva-darśinaḥ. If you want to understand the truth, you must approach a guru, you must inquire submissively and you must render service to the spiritual master. In this way, the spiritual master will reveal knowledge unto you because he has seen the truth. So, when we are discussing the divine leela of Radha and Kṛṣṇa, we are discussing the ultimate, the summum bonum of the Absolute Truth.We are discussing the highest knowledge even within the realm of transcendental knowledge. So, therefore, we must approach according to how the great acharyas are teaching us. And in this way, we can get again actual realization of the divine mercy of Srimati Radharani and Lord Sri Kṛṣṇa. So here it is being described that Kṛṣṇa is the Param Brahman. Arjuna has said paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ idam uttamam, that Kṛṣṇa You are the Supreme Brahman, you are the supreme purifier, you are the abode in which everyone is residing. Everything was within you and you are within everything.

Kṛṣṇa sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇam. He is the cause of all causes.And it is being described here by Swarup Damodara goswami that, that one supreme absolute truth for the sake of eternally enjoying divine pleasure, expands himself into two forms- Radha and Kṛṣṇa. Radharani is considered the hladini Shakti. hladini means the pleasure giving potency.This means that there is no difference between Radha and Kṛṣṇa. Some people think that Radharani is the greatest devotee. But that in itself is very inaccurate. Radharani is Kṛṣṇa. She is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. All the potencies and glories of Kṛṣṇa are similarly within the personality of Srimati Radharani.But, although they are one, they have separated themselves into two for the purpose of ecstatic, loving reciprocation, because in order for there to be pleasure of love, there must be a lover and a beloved. Therefore, Kṛṣṇa is the supreme enjoyer and Srimati Radharani is the supreme enjoyed. Kṛṣṇa is the supreme master and Srimati Radharani is the supreme lover of that Supreme Master.Therefore, bhakti or devotional service is the divine property of Srimati Radharani alone and it is only through her mercy, through her grace that anyone can approach Kṛṣṇa, because as Kṛṣṇa says, bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya aham evaṁ-vidho ’rjuna. It is only by pure devotion that I can be approached. It is by no other means.

Try to understand that the loving affairs of Radha and Kṛṣṇa, there is nothing material. How is it possible? After all, great Brahmacharis and sanyasis, throughout history have given up all material pleasures, have given up their families, have given up their wealth, have performed severe tapasya, simply for a glimpse of brahman, or the realization of this spiritual existence of the Lord.

We see even great kings like Bharat maharaj, how they renounced their kingdom and they became very, very simple, living in the forest performing great tapasya. Simply to try to understand brahman or the spiritual existence of the Lord. So here we find Kṛṣṇa who is Param Brahma, the Supreme Brahman, do you think he will be attracted to any material pleasure? This is very crazy assumption. Therefore, the pleasure of Sri Kṛṣṇa is on a purely spiritual divine platform. And therefore, we must understand the relationship of Radha- Kṛṣṇa, as the ultimate expression of love of God for himself. And as the aspiring devotees who are trying to gain entrance into the loving service of the Lord, we must approach very, very carefully and very much according to the authorized instructions of the great acharyas.

This evening, we will celebrate Radhastami. This evening, we will have a nice festival, we will describe the nature of the appearance of Radharani and we will also discuss the nature of the land of Vrindavan which is the, Radharani is Vrindavaneshvari, she is the queen and controller of Vrindavan. But this morning for just a few minutes, I believe it is most important, far more important than hearing the pastimes of Radha and Kṛṣṇa, to understand the proper approach to Radha and Kṛṣṇa.

Some people, they go to Vrindavan and they just.. Jai Radhe, Jai Kṛṣṇa, and they think they will reach Kṛṣṇa in this way. This is not the process. Kṛṣṇa is not so cheap. You are talking about the highest realization of Godhead, millions of times higher than realization of the Brahma Jyoti. We must understand that there are various levels of bhakti. Sadhana Bhakti is divided into two parts, Vidhi bhakti and Raga bhakti. Vidhi bhakti means you do not have love of God and if you think you do, you are completely insane. Love of God means you cannot, you cannot have love of God and even a tinge of particle of material attachment in your heart. Real love of God is only when we become completely purified from the bodily conception of life. So, if we are not on that platform, we must understand that we are in the platform of Vidhi bhakti. That means the process of devotional service to regulative principles. That means you must accept a spiritual master and you must be disciplined by him. He must be disciplined by following the regulative principles and by performing tapasya under his instruction.

Kṛṣṇa says in Gita rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu viṣayān indriyaiś caran. That by following the regulative principles of freedom, one can obtain the complete mercy of the Lord.

Now, after becoming completely purified.. (Unclear) bhakti one attains the state which is called raga bhakti, which means spontaneous, some spontaneous enthusiasm to serve Radha and Kṛṣṇa, where there is no more any material desires to impede our devotion to the Lord. This is a very elevated stage of consciousness. It is that stage where paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate, you are simply not attracted to any of the temporary manifestations of the world. You are simply attracted to the service of Radha and Kṛṣṇa. By practicing devotional service on this platform of raga bhakti for some time, then only can you enter into the realm of Prema bhakti or pure love of God.

So, as we emphasize very strongly, especially here in India, we cannot take this subject matter cheaply, we must approach it as a great science if we want the real results. Some people we find, they dress themselves up as gopis or Srimati Radharani and think by playing the role of gopis, then we will develop the sentiment of the gopis, but nowhere in the sastras and nowhere in the teachings of the bonafide acharya is this ever accepted. In fact, the real acharyas reject this conception. You cannot simply pretend to be a gopi. First you have to, gopis are the highest position of pure love. How can you pretend to be on that platform, either you are a gopi or you are not a gopi. Therefore, a sane person who is properly following the real system, he becomes the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant of the gopis of Vrindavan, which means he learns how to properly control his senses and engage all of his activities in the mission of his spiritual master. He takes the humblest position. After all, you can only approach God through other humility.

Does humility mean pretending you are on the ultimate position or to accept the lowest position? Which one? Therefore, to pretend that we are gopis transgresses the principle of humility.

We are trying to play the role of the ultimate devotees of the Lord before we can even control our senses properly. Even those who have attained mukti who are totally liberated from our material desires, they have no access to the feelings of the gopis. We can only attain the feelings of the gopis through utter surrender to the feet of the Lord. When you achieve the blessings in the mercy of the Lord and his servants, then can we enter into these spiritual feelings not by our own endeavors. So therefore, we should know that if we truly really want to gain the blessings of Srimati Radharani then we must take the most insignificant position of being the menial servant of the servant of the servant.

In fact, the six goswamis, they were the manjaris of Vrindavan who descended into this world. Nowhere do we find the six goswami saying, “oh, I saw Kṛṣṇa, oh I am seeing Kṛṣṇa, Yes, Kṛṣṇa was dancing for me, Kṛṣṇa was playing his flute for me.” The six goswamis are always praying with tears in their eyes “Where is Radha? Where is Kṛṣṇa?” Do you think it's seeing Radha and Kṛṣṇa is so cheap, that they just appear to you? The price of seeing Radha and Kṛṣṇa is that you must be immersed in separation and longing for them to such an extent that, that separation totally, totally pervades your heart and you cannot think of anything else. Therefore, those who are actually on the path according to the great acharyas rather than talking about how I have seen Radha and Kṛṣṇa, they are trying to cultivate the intense and genuine feelings of separation from Radha and Kṛṣṇa. It is only then, that Radha and Kṛṣṇa actually reveal themselves to you.

To see the pastimes of Radha and Kṛṣṇa are not simply a subject for entertainment. Most people in this land of India, they come to pravachan more to be entertained than they do to be purified. Therefore, when Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur was giving Srimad Bhagavatam class for 30 days in Vrindavan, everyone was thinking he is the highest saint there is, how much nectar we will receive by hearing Kṛṣṇa and Radharani’s leela and he simply spoke for the 30 days on the first verse, explaining the difference between matter and spirit. And most of the people went away because they were disappointed, he is not giving us the nectar, Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati, he very purposely did this. Until you…until your senses are purified you cannot taste the nectar. Until your senses are completely purified, Radha Kṛṣṇa’s leela is just a story that's completely way over your head. It's like describing PhD mathematics to a kindergartener. They might, it might sound very nice, but you have no conception of what it is. Therefore, to hear the divine leela Radha and Kṛṣṇa certainly purifying, but it must be very much in conjunction with the basic principles of Kṛṣṇa consciousness. Therefore, we find that Srila Prabhupada, he is translating for the world, these very, very high subjects of the verses of the Chaitanya Charitamrita. But he is also giving elaborate purports which are describing the exact position and condition of our consciousness and how to elevate it.

So therefore, to actually be the real servant of Srimati Radharani and to really attain her divine blessings, we must become the humble servants of the servants of the servants of the servants.

If we really truly want to gain her divine blessings, we must approach her through the via medium of the recognized sadhus and the spiritual master and gradually step by step by step, they teach us how to become free from ahankara, false ego. Even the great devotee Narottam Das Thakur, he was living in Vrindavan. He was constantly exhibiting the symptoms of ecstatic love. But how did he pray? viṣaya chāriyā kabe śuddha ha ‘be mana, kabe hāma herabo śrī-bṛndāban. These are the greatest acharyas how they pray. They are not saying, “oh, I saw Kṛṣṇa in dream last night. Oh, Kṛṣṇa danced for me, oh Kṛṣṇa.” The great acharyas, they are praying, they are longing, their eyes are filled with tears, their hearts are just.. just like fountains of emotion and what is their prayer? “When, oh when will I be free from all material desires, only then will I see Vrindavan, only then can I.. can I even approach the divine leela of Radha and Kṛṣṇa?

he radhe vraja-devike ca lalite he nanda-suno kutah and the six goswamis, they are praying, “Where is Radharani, where are you Kṛṣṇa? Are you at Govardhan, are you at Bamsi vata, my heart is simply longing in separation for you.”

So, we must cultivate these feelings of separation. We must cultivate the feeling of yearning and longing for the divine service of the divine couple Sri Sri Radha and Kṛṣṇa and these divine feelings of separation, they awaken within our heart only by the mercy of Srimati Radharani. And that Mercy is coming through her representatives. And therefore, on this holy day of Radhastami,

we should be very much sober and at the same time joyful to (unclear)… and the order of Guru, a system in his divine service of giving pleasure to Sri Sri Radha Gopinath.

On this day, of course, we can pray to Srimati Radharani, she is the giver of devotion, she is the giver of love. Our prayer should be that “Srimati Radharani! Oh beloved of Sri Kṛṣṇa, by your causeless and infinite mercy, by your kindness, kindly allow me to gradually become purified, by engaging as the servant of the servant of the servant of your devoted servants.” There is no prayer that is more pleasing to Srimati Radharani than this and if we offer this prayer with a deep and sincere desire to actually live according to this prayer, that is the greatest gift, that is the greatest offering we can offer on this auspicious day of Radhastami.

So everyone please come this evening we will have a nice celebration. The bhajans and kirtan will begin at four o'clock. Hare Kṛṣṇa!
